title: Charmed Life — 1 (Fragment 1)
sourceText:
Chapter 1

1

C 
 AT C HANT ADMIRED his elder sister Gwendolen. She was a witch. He admired her and he clung to her. Great changes came about in their lives and left him no one else to cling to.

The first great change came about when their parents took them out for a day trip down the river in a paddle steamer. They set out in great style, Gwendolen and her mother in white dresses with ribbons, Cat and his father in prickly blue-serge Sunday suits. It was a hot day. The steamer was crammed with other people in holiday clothes, talking, laughing, eating whelks with thin slices of white bread and butter, while the paddleboat steam organ wheezed out popular tunes so that no one could hear themselves talk.

In fact the steamer was too crowded and too old. Something went wrong with the steering. The whole laughing, whelk-eating, Sunday-dressed crowd was swept away in the current from the dam. They hit one of the posts which were supposed to stop people being swept away, and the paddle steamer, being old, simply broke into pieces. Cat remembered the organ playing and the paddles beating the blue sky. Clouds of steam screamed from broken pipes and drowned the screams from the crowd, as every single person aboard was swept away through the dam. It was a terrible accident. The papers called it the Saucy Nancy Disaster. The ladies in their clinging skirts were quite unable to swim. The men in tight blue serge were very little better off. But Gwendolen was a witch, so she could not drown. And Cat, who flung his arms around Gwendolen when the boat hit the post, survived too. There were very few other survivors.

The whole country was shocked by it. The paddleboat company and the town of Wolvercote between them paid for the funerals. Gwendolen and Cat were given heavy black clothes at public expense, and rode behind the procession of hearses in a carriage pulled by black horses with black plumes on their heads. The other survivors rode with them. Cat looked at them and wondered if they were witches and warlocks, but he never found out. The Mayor of Wolvercote had set up a Fund for the survivors. Money poured in from all over the country. All the other survivors took their share and went away to start new lives elsewhere. Only Cat and Gwendolen were left and, since nobody could discover any of their relations, they stayed in Wolvercote.

They became celebrities for a time. Everyone was very kind. Everyone said what beautiful little orphans they were. It was true. They were both fair and pale, with blue eyes, and looked good in black. Gwendolen was very pretty, and tall for her age. Cat was small for his age. Gwendolen was very motherly to Cat, and people were touched. Cat did not mind. It made up a little for the empty, lost way he was feeling. Ladies gave him cake and toys. Town Councillors came and asked how he was getting on; and the Mayor called and patted him on the head. The Mayor explained that the money from the Fund was being put into a Trust for them until they were grown up. Meanwhile, the town would pay for their education and upbringing.

words.definition: Held on tightly to something or someone.
words.example: He admired her and he clung to her.
words.exampleText: He admired her and he clung to her.

words.definition: Having small, sharp points; causing irritation or difficulty.
words.example: Cat and his father in prickly blue-serge Sunday suits.
words.exampleText: Cat and his father in prickly blue-serge Sunday suits.

words.definition: A ship, boat, or other vessel powered by steam.
words.example: The first great change came about when their parents took them out for a day trip down the river in a paddle steamer.
words.exampleText: ...took them out for a day trip down the river in a paddle steamer.

words.definition: A sea snail that is often eaten as seafood.
words.example: ...laughing, eating whelks with thin slices of white bread and butter.
words.exampleText: laughing, eating whelks with thin slices of white bread and butter

words.definition: A large musical instrument with rows of pipes played using a keyboard.
words.example: The paddleboat steam organ wheezed out popular tunes.
words.exampleText: The paddleboat steam organ wheezed out popular tunes.

words.definition: Made a noise like a breathy or rattling sound, especially when breathing or producing sound.
words.example: The paddleboat steam organ wheezed out popular tunes.
words.exampleText: The paddleboat steam organ wheezed out popular tunes.

words.definition: Filled with a large number of people or things.
words.example: In fact the steamer was too crowded and too old.
words.exampleText: In fact the steamer was too crowded and too old.

words.definition: The act or mechanism of guiding or controlling the direction of a vehicle or vessel.
words.example: Something went wrong with the steering.
words.exampleText: Something went wrong with the steering.

words.definition: A flow of water in a river, sea, or other body of water.
words.example: The whole laughing, whelk-eating, Sunday-dressed crowd was swept away in the current from the dam.
words.exampleText: ...was swept away in the current from the dam.

words.definition: Upright pieces of wood or metal fixed in the ground to support or mark something.
words.example: They hit one of the posts which were supposed to stop people being swept away.
words.exampleText: They hit one of the posts...

words.definition: To die under water by suffocating.
words.example: Clouds of steam screamed from broken pipes and drowned the screams from the crowd.
words.exampleText: Clouds of steam ... drowned the screams from the crowd.

words.definition: Continued to live or exist after a dangerous event.
words.example: Cat, who flung his arms around Gwendolen when the boat hit the post, survived too.
words.exampleText: Cat ... survived too.

words.definition: A group of people moving forward in an orderly way, often for ceremonial occasions.
words.example: They rode behind the procession of hearses in a carriage.
words.exampleText: rode behind the procession of hearses in a carriage.

words.definition: Vehicles used to carry coffins at funerals.
words.example: They rode behind the procession of hearses.
words.exampleText: They rode behind the procession of hearses.

words.definition: Feather-like decorative ornaments, often worn on hats or helmets.
words.example: ...black horses with black plumes on their heads.
words.exampleText: black horses with black plumes on their heads.

words.definition: Men who practise magic; male witches.
words.example: Cat looked at them and wondered if they were witches and warlocks.
words.exampleText: wondered if they were witches and warlocks.

words.definition: A sum of money saved or made available for a particular purpose.
words.example: The Mayor of Wolvercote had set up a Fund for the survivors.
words.exampleText: set up a Fund for the survivors.

words.definition: Flowed in quickly or abundantly.
words.example: Money poured in from all over the country.
words.exampleText: Money poured in from all over the country.

words.definition: Famous people, often admired by many.
words.example: They became celebrities for a time.
words.exampleText: They became celebrities for a time.

words.definition: Children whose parents are dead.
words.example: Everyone said what beautiful little orphans they were.
words.exampleText: beautiful little orphans they were.

words.definition: Members of a council, especially local government officials.

words.exampleText: Town Councillors came and asked.

words.definition: Money saved or made available for a specific purpose.
words.example: The money from the Fund was being put into a Trust for them.
words.exampleText: money from the Fund was being put into a Trust.

words.definition: An arrangement to hold property or money for the benefit of someone else.
words.example: The money from the Fund was being put into a Trust for them until they were grown up.

words.definition: The treatment and training received from parents during childhood.
words.example: The town would pay for their education and upbringing.
words.exampleText: pay for their education and upbringing.

words.definition: Threw or moved something with force or suddenly.

words.exampleText: He flung his arms around Gwendolen.

words.definition: Famous and admired.

words.exampleText: They became celebrities for a time.

words.definition: Emotionally affected; moved by feelings.
